text:
Once upon a time, in a small seaside town called Willow Creek, there was an eccentric old botanist named Harriet. She had planted seeds in forgotten corners of town and tended mysterious bonsCs. One sunny afternoon, she found an old oak bean stalk fluttering alone among the others.

She poured herself coffee and picked up the stalk. It snapped like twện. Interested, she planted it. Come spring, unusual beans sprouted. They grew taller fast. One day, a purple leaf grew wider than the windowsill.

Young worms ate it. She caught them. Study them. Maybe they'd know something. She named them Wormy. Months later, beans grew fruits.

They were purple. Strange. Harvested. Pills. She took them. Better dreams. Friends called. Heard strange stories.

When harvest time came again, she didn'd just pick. She'd shake. Cure.

Neighbors moaned. She smiled. Maybe.

A knock. Door.

"Dotty?"

She rose
